channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Advocacy groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending Swiss criminal probe of woman's death
item.description: Advocacy groups behind a so-called suicide capsule say they have suspended the process of taking applications for its use as a criminal investigation into its first use in Switzerland is completed.
item.dc:creator: Jamey Keaten, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Automation takes center stage in port negotiations
item.description: Dockworkers and longshoremen with the International Longshoremen's Association (ILA) are underscoring a fear being felt across various industries: Will robots replace them at their jobs? The ILA's three-day strike and ongoing labor negotiations have brought the fight against automation technology
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Bay Area poll: 7 in 10 residents say the region’s quality of life is getting worse
item.description: Residents are deeply skeptical of the tech industry and support stricter penalties for theft and drug crimes.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Varian, The Mercury News

item.title: Is the stock market in an AI bubble?
item.description: When it comes to AI investments, “a lot of money is going to get wasted,” MIT professor Daron Acemoglu told Bloomberg.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Node.js previews network inspection support
item.description: Node.js v20.18.0, a just-introduced update to the Long-Term Support (LTS) version of the popular asynchronous, event-driven JavaScript runtime, features experimental network inspection support. Introduced October 3, Node.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 10 tech tips and tricks everyone should know
item.description: There are a handful of ways you can make your tech experience less annoying, including through fixing autocorrect, turning off certain notifications and by creating folders on your iPhone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alphabet’s Waymo will expand its robotaxi fleet with Hyundai EVs
item.description: Alphabet’s Waymo will add electric vehicles from South Korea’s Hyundai Motor to its robotaxi fleet, the companies said on Friday, as Waymo expands in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.94 improves file search
item.description: The September 2024 release of Microsoft’s Visual Studio Code editor, version 1.94, features improvements for finding files using the File Explorer. The upgrade also introduces the ability to run Python tests with coverage.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk’s friendship with Diddy examined after he posted about boy’s alleged abuse
item.description: Sean 'Diddy' Combs' mentee Aubrey O'Day and others called out Musk for trying to distance himself from allegations that the music mogul sexually abused multiple victims, including a 9-year-old boy.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: Google CEO eyeing electricity from nuclear plants for its data centers
item.description: Google CEO Sundar Pichai is considering using electricity from nuclear plants to power his company’s data centers, as the tech giant contends with the expansive energy demands of artificial intelligence (AI).
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Musk donated millions to GOP causes as early as 2022: Reports
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk donated millions of dollars to Republican causes as early as 2022, much earlier than previously known, according to media reports.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Windows 11 Insider Previews: What’s in the latest build?
item.description: The Windows 11 2023 Update has been released, but behind the scenes, Microsoft is constantly working to improve the newest version of Windows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenAI demands investors shun rivals such as Anthropic, Elon Musk’s xAI
item.description: OpenAI has raised $6.6 billion from investors like Thrive Capital and Tiger Global, but the AI company also sought assurances that investors would avoid funding five competing firms, according to a Reuters report.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Women of color running for Congress disproportionately targeted by offensive speech on X: Report
item.description: Congressional candidates who are women, specifically those of color, are more likely than other candidates to be targeted by offensive or hate speech on the social platform X, according to a new study.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: 4 in 10 Americans think generative AI will reduce jobs: Report
item.description: Nearly 4 in 10 Americans in a new analysis believe generative artificial intelligence (AI) could diminish the number of available jobs as the technology advances.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Beware of fraudsters posing as government officials trying to steal your cash
item.description: CISA warns of scammers impersonating its officials. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson urges people to be alert and verify contacts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tesla posts first quarterly increase in deliveries, but shares slump with investors hoping for more
item.description: Low interest financing, sweet lease deals, price cuts and free charging boosted Tesla’s global deliveries in the third quarter, the first increase this year for the electric vehicle maker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: DoD agencies confront zero trust challenges, misunderstandings ahead of 2026 deadline
item.description: The Defense Department’s zero trust framework is acting as a catalyst, driving mission owners to industry in search of zero trust solutions The post DoD agencies confront zero trust challenges, misunderstandings ahead of 2026 deadline first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: 8 easy ways to transfer files between Windows and your phone
item.description: We live in a multi-device world. At a minimum, you almost certainly have both a computer and a phone — and potentially many more devices than that! And when it comes to transferring files between your Windows PC and whatever phone you’re using, you’re absolutely spoiled for choice.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: iPhone's satellite messaging: A lifeline in Hurricane Helene's aftermath
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how iOS 18’s satellite messaging helps isolated residents seek help in the aftermath of Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft releases official OpenAI library for .NET
item.description: Microsoft has released its official OpenAI library for .NET, with the goal of ensuring a smooth, reliable integration experience for developers working with OpenAI and Azure OpenAI services in .NET applications. Announced October 1, the now-stable library follows a beta release published in June.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why determining the cost of cloud services cuts across an entire agency
item.description: Do you know your true cloud costs? The post Why determining the cost of cloud services cuts across an entire agency first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network
